optionList.content: Washing with soap to promote emulsification
Use of hot water to destroy bacteria
Splashing water onto the arms
Applying alcohol-based hand rubs to remove visible soiling
Washing with soap to promote emulsification
Use of hot water to destroy bacteria
Splashing water onto the arms
Applying alcohol-based hand rubs to remove visible soiling

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is the most effective handwashing technique?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Soaps and detergents are used to remove bacteria because of their ability to lower the surface tension of water and act as emulsifying agents.
Option 2:Hot water may lead to skin irritation or burns. Using warm water is recommended for effective handwashing.
Option 3:Splashing water can cause contamination of the arms and wrists. Hence, it is better to avoid splashing water.
Option 4:Use alcohol-based hand rubs when the hands are not visibly soiled and when certain pathogens are suspected.

optionList.content: Using sterile forceps, rather than sterile gloves, to handle a sterile item
Touching the outside wrapper of the sterilized material without sterile gloves
Placing a sterile object on the edge of the sterile field
Pouring out a small amount of solution (15 to 30 mL) before pouring the solution into a sterile container
Using sterile forceps, rather than sterile gloves, to handle a sterile item
Touching the outside wrapper of the sterilized material without sterile gloves
Placing a sterile object on the edge of the sterile field
Pouring out a small amount of solution (15 to 30 mL) before pouring the solution into a sterile container

studentQuestionArray.content: What constitutes a break in sterile techniques while preparing a sterile field for a dressing change?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Using sterile forceps prevents contamination; hence, it is a good sterile technique.
Option 2:The outer wrapper of the sterilized material is considered to be contaminated. Therefore, it should not be touched with sterile gloves.
Option 3:The edges of a sterile field are considered to be contaminated. When sterile items are allowed to come in contact with the edges of the field, they become contaminated.
Option 4:The outer portion of the solution is in contact with contaminated air. Hence, pouring a portion of the solution before pouring the rest into a sterile container is an ideal sterile technique.

optionList.content: Infection in the oral mucosa
Food poisoning
Cholera and typhoid fever
Bacteremia
Infection in the oral mucosa
Food poisoning
Cholera and typhoid fever
Bacteremia

studentQuestionArray.content: What type of disease is caused by the bacteria Salmonella enteritidis?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Candida albicans causes infections in oralmucosa known as thrush. Salmonella enteritidis bacteria does not cause such oral infections.
Option 2:Salmonella enteritidis causes food poisoning, which is known as salmonellosis.
Option 3:Cholera and typhoid fever are caused by pathogens found in the drinking water. These diseases are not caused by Salmonella enteritidis bacteria.
Option 4:The clinical identification of any bacterial strain in the blood is defined as bacteremia.

optionList.content: Washing and drying hands before wearing gloves
Covering the mouth and nose with gloved hands when sneezing
Wearing a respiratory mask
Wearing a hair cover
Washing and drying hands before wearing gloves
Covering the mouth and nose with gloved hands when sneezing
Wearing a respiratory mask
Wearing a hair cover

studentQuestionArray.content: Which precautionary measure taken by the nurse to prevent spread of infection may have just the opposite effect and contaminate the surroundings?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:It is important to wash and dry hands before wearing gloves to prevent the multiplication of microbes in the moisture locked hands.
Option 2:If a nurse covers the mouth and the nose with gloved hands while sneezing, and touches the surroundings with the same hands, the microbes that were transferred to the gloves while sneezing can contaminate the surroundings.
Option 3:Wearing a respiratory mask helps prevent the spear of air-borne infections, and is a mandatory practice when caring for clients in isolation.
Option 4:Wearing a hair cover prevents the spread of microbes that may be sticking to the hair to the outside environment.

optionList.content: The nurse must utilize standard precautions.
The nurse must wear a mask when working within of 3 feet of the client.
The nurse must perform a surgical scrub.
The nurse must dress in sterile surgical attire.
The nurse must utilize standard precautions.
The nurse must wear a mask when working within of 3 feet of the client.
The nurse must perform a surgical scrub.
The nurse must dress in sterile surgical attire.

studentQuestionArray.content: What is most important regarding safety measures a nurse should take while assessing a client who is in droplet isolation?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:While caring for a client in droplet isolation, a nurse needs to observe standard precautions as well as maintain a distance of 3 feet to prevent the spread of infection.
Option 2:The nurse must wear a mask when working within 3 feet of the client while assessing a client in droplet isolation, in order to prevent the spread of the disease.
Option 3:A surgical scrub is performed before the surgery to remove pathogens. This is not a procedure that needs to be followed, while assessing a client in droplet isolation.
Option 4:The nurse must dress in sterile surgical outfit if engaged in surgery. This measure is not required in case of a client in droplet isolation.

Nurse B

Nurse D
Nurse A
Nurse B
Nurse C
Nurse D

studentQuestionArray.content: Four different nurses are caring for four different clients. Which nurse's action would provide a clean client environment?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales


Option 1:Nurse A should avoid stocking the needed supplies in the client's room as these would lead to contamination.

Option 2:Nurse B should not allow the client to use items brought from the client's home, as they are considered contaminated.

Option 3:Nurse C's action would provide the client with a clean environment. The stethoscope should be cleaned before using with alcohol as they are often contaminated with Staphylococcus aureus.

Option 4:Nurse D should not return the contaminated supply to the cart and should dispose of the supply according to the agency policy.

optionList.content: Gown
N-95 respirator
Face shield
Hair covers
Gown
N-95 respirator
Face shield
Hair covers

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is the appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) that the nurse should wear when caring for a client with tuberculosis (TB)?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should wear a gown in situations where there is excessive wound drainage, or other discharge from the body.
Option 2:The nurse should wear an N-95 respirator when caring for clients with TB. An N-95 respirator prevents airborne transmission of the pathogens.
Option 3:The nurse should wear a face shield when splashing might occur to protect the entire facial area.
Option 4:The nurse should wear hair cover when there is a possibility of splashing and spraying.

optionList.content: The nurse wears nonsterile gloves.
The nurse labels the feeding container with the client's name, date, and time.
The nurse uses the unsterilized calibrated cap of a medicine bottle to measure the quantity of the feeding solution to be administered.
The nurse replaces the feeding equipment after 24 hours.
The nurse wears nonsterile gloves.
The nurse labels the feeding container with the client's name, date, and time.
The nurse uses the unsterilized calibrated cap of a medicine bottle to measure the quantity of the feeding solution to be administered.
The nurse replaces the feeding equipment after 24 hours.

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse is giving enteral feedings to a client in the post-surgical unit. Which action of the nurse can contaminate the feeding solution and put the client at risk for a bacterial infection?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse must wear nonsterile gloves when administering enteral feedings to clients.
Option 2:The nurse should always label the feeding container with the client's name, date and time.
Option 3:When administering enteral feeding to a client, the nurse must always use sterile equipment that is specifically meant for feeding.
Option 4:The feeding equipment should always be replaced after 24 hours. Sterile solutions can be hung up to 24 hours, while nonsterile solutions can only be hung up to 4 hours.

optionList.content: Placing the used needle in the supply cart
Recapping the used needle with both hands
Breaking the used needle and then disposing of it in the needle reciprocal
Using one-handed scoop technique
Placing the used needle in the supply cart

Breaking the used needle and then disposing of it in the needle reciprocal
Using one-handed scoop technique

studentQuestionArray.content: A clinician is administering an anesthesia injection to a client before a minor surgery. Which measure, regarding disposal of the needle, is correct?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The clinician should place the needle in a puncture-resistant container for disposal after use.
Option 2:The clinician should never recap the used needle.
Option 3:The clinician should not break the used needle.
Option 4:The clinician should use the one-handed scoop technique or mechanical device designed for holding the needle sheath.

optionList.content: The nurse should provide the client with adequate nutrition.
The nurse should provide fluids at room temperature.
The nurse should inspect surgical dressings for drainage and odor.
The nurse should monitor the client's vital signs, especially the temperature.
The nurse should provide the client with adequate nutrition.
The nurse should provide fluids at room temperature.
The nurse should inspect surgical dressings for drainage and odor.
The nurse should monitor the client's vital signs, especially the temperature.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is the best intervention by the nurse to ensure that a client in postsurgical care does not develop dehiscence at the incision site?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Dehiscence at the incision site is caused by poor nutritional status, among other things. The nurse should ensure that the client gets adequate nutrition.
Option 2:The nurse should provide fluids at room temperature to avoid any possible complication of abdominal distention. This is unrelated with dehiscence at the incision site.
Option 3:The nurse should inspect the surgical dressings for drainage and odor to prevent wound infections.
Option 4:To keep a check on wound infections, the nurse should monitor the client's vital signs, especially temperature.

optionList.content: Neutralization
Phagocytosis
Agglutination
Activation of complement and inflammation
Neutralization
Phagocytosis
Agglutination
Activation of complement and inflammation

studentQuestionArray.content: Which method is used by antibodies to signal leukocytes to engulf and destroy the bacteria or other foreign materials entering the human body?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Neutralization is the process that occurs when an acid and a base come in contact with each other to make salt and water.
Option 2:Antibodies signal leukocytes to phagocytize the pathogens to which the antibodies are bound. Phagocytosis is the process by which a cell engulfs materials either to destroy it, to feed on it, or to get information from it.
Option 3:Agglutination is a reaction in which bacteria is suspended in a liquid collected into clumps. This occurs especially as a response to a specific antibody.
Option 4:Antibodies trigger the complement cascade and stimulate the release of inflammatory chemicals to destroy the antigen.

optionList.content: You should wash your hands frequently and bathe regularly.
Make a list of necessary immunizations and get yourself vaccinated.
Keep body activities to a minimum.
Eat healthy food and follow a proper diet to maintain nutritional balance.
You should wash your hands frequently and bathe regularly.
Make a list of necessary immunizations and get yourself vaccinated.
Keep body activities to a minimum.
Eat healthy food and follow a proper diet to maintain nutritional balance.

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse teaching a client about enhancing the body's defensive mechanisms to fight infections. Which statement indicates a need for further training?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Washing hands and bathing regularly helps the client maintain good hygiene. It maintains the body's defenses against infections.
Option 2:Getting immunized through vaccinations will protect the client against many infectious diseases.
Option 3:Minimal body activity hampers the body's defenses against infections.
Option 4:Healthy eating habits and adequate nutrition enhance the body's immunity, thus helping it fight infections.

optionList.content: The stage where the signs and symptoms of a particular disease are recognized
The stage in between the exposure to pathogens and the first indication of symptoms
The stage which is categorized by the appearance of imprecise symptoms
The stage where the signs and symptoms of the disease start to weaken
The stage where the signs and symptoms of a particular disease are recognized
The stage in between the exposure to pathogens and the first indication of symptoms
The stage which is categorized by the appearance of imprecise symptoms
The stage where the signs and symptoms of the disease start to weaken

studentQuestionArray.content: Which statement describes the incubation stage?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Illness is the stage where the signs and symptoms of a particular disease are recognized. Thus, this is not referred to as incubation stage.
Option 2:Incubation is the phase in between the exposure to pathogens and the first indication of symptoms in the body.
Option 3:Prodromal stage is categorized by the appearance of imprecise symptoms that does not happen during the incubation stage.
Option 4:Decline is the stage where the signs and symptoms of the disease start to weaken and the number of pathogens is reduced. Hence, this is not known as incubation stage.

optionList.content: Keep conversation around the sterile field to a minimum.
Handle sterile equipment only when wearing clean gloves.
Stay 1 foot away from the sterile field even when wearing sterile attire.
Set up the sterile field well in advance of the procedure.
Keep conversation around the sterile field to a minimum.

Stay 1 foot away from the sterile field even when wearing sterile attire.
Set up the sterile field well in advance of the procedure.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which measure is best for a nurse to take to maintain a sterile field?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should keep conversation to a minimum around the sterile field to minimize the spread of droplets.
Option 2:The nurse should handle sterile equipment only when wearing sterile gloves.
Option 3:The nurse should stay 1 foot away from nonsterile areas when wearing sterile attire.
Option 4:The nurse should set up the sterile field just before the procedure.

optionList.content: I should open the flap away from me when l open the package.
I should pull the final flap toward me when l open the package.
I should readjust the sterile area after opening the package.
I should avoid fanning the sterile drape.
I should open the flap away from me when l open the package.
I should pull the final flap toward me when l open the package.
I should readjust the sterile area after opening the package.
I should avoid fanning the sterile drape.

studentQuestionArray.content: A registered nurse is teaching a novice nurse about how to set up a sterile field before a surgery. Which statement made by the novice nurse indicates the need for further teaching?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should open the flap of the package away from him or her. This action prevents crossing an unsterile arm over the sterile items. If an unsterile arm is moved over the sterile field, it will not be considered sterile.
Option 2:The final flap should be pulled toward the nurse to avoid contamination.
Option 3:Readjusting the sterile area after opening the package places the unsterile areas of the wrapper in the sterile filed. Hence, the sterile area should not be readjusted after opening the package.
Option 4:The nurse should avoid fanning of the sterile drape. A border of 1 inch around the sterile drape is considered unsterile. Hence, fanning would lead to contamination.

optionList.content: N-95 respirator
Face shield
Face mask
Shoe cover
N-95 respirator
Face shield
Face mask
Shoe cover

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse is preparing a client diagnosed with tuberculosis for surgery. Which device should the nurse wear to prevent herself or himself from becoming infected with the disease?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse who is caring for clients in a tuberculosis unit should wear an N-95 respirator. The device prevents the transmission of airborne diseases, such as tuberculosis.
Option 2:A face shield is used to protect the nurse's entire face from crown to chin while caring for clients. The device is used to protect the nurse's face from blood splashing and respiratory droplets.
Option 3:Face masks are used to prevent the transmission of pathogens through mucous membranes when the nurse is in contact with the respiratory secretions of the client.
Option 4:A shoe cover is worn by the nurse when there is a potential for contamination of the nurse's shoes with the client's body fluid.

optionList.content: It could harbor microbes.
It could make the nails brittle.
It could cause unintentional poisoning.
It can cause contact dermatitis.
It could harbor microbes.
It could make the nails brittle.
It could cause unintentional poisoning.
It can cause contact dermatitis.

studentQuestionArray.content: The registered nurse notices that a novice nurse in the perioperative unit is wearing nail polish. The nurse advises the novice nurse to remove the nail polish completely. Which is the nurse’s greatest concern related to nail polish?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Nurses should not wear while engaging in direct client care to maintain hand hygiene. Nail polish acts as a reservoir for microbes and increases the client's risk of exposure to them.
Option 2:Nail polish may make the nails brittle, but that is not the primary objective of the registered nurse in asking the novice nurse to remove her nail polish.
Option 3:Nail polish poisoning occurs only when it is swallowed. The application of nail polish does not cause unintentional poisoning.
Option 4:Nail polish does not cause contact dermatitis.

optionList.content: Standing 1 foot away from the sterile field
Bending down to pick up an instrument that has accidentally fallen
Checking the sterility of all items that are to be added to the sterile field using a sterilization indicator
Discarding items that do not seem to be properly sterilized
Standing 1 foot away from the sterile field
Bending down to pick up an instrument that has accidentally fallen
Checking the sterility of all items that are to be added to the sterile field using a sterilization indicator
Discarding items that do not seem to be properly sterilized

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action performed by a nurse can contaminate a sterile field?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:When preparing a sterile field, it is advised to stand at least 1 foot away from the preparation site.
Option 2:When preparing a sterile field, a nurse must never bend down because any area below the sterile plane is considered contaminated.
Option 3:All items that are to be added to a sterile field must be checked using a sterilization indicator.
Option 4:The nurse must immediately discard an item whose sterility is questionable.

I should open the sterile package's flap towards me first.
l can adjust the sterile area after opening the sterile package.

I should pick up the sterile drape by the comer.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which statement made by the nurse is correct regarding how to set up a sterile field?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should first open the sterile package's flap away from him or her to prevent passing an unsterile arm over the sterile items.
Option 2:Adjusting the sterile area after opening the sterile package will shift the sterile field and would place the unsterile areas of the wrapper on the sterile field.
Option 3:The nurse should hold the edge of the package and peel it back to avoid contamination of the sterile drape present inside it.
Option 4:The sterile drape should be picked up by the corner to avoid contamination.

optionList.content: Foot traffic in the sterile area should be limited.
Staff in sterile garb should be advised to stay 1 foot away from the sterile area.
Staff can reach over the sterile area when wearing a sterile glove.
Sterile equipment should be handled only if the nurse is wearing a sterile glove.
Staff can touch the inside of the sterile contents wrapper with bare hands.
Foot traffic in the sterile area should be limited.
Staff in sterile garb should be advised to stay 1 foot away from the sterile area.
Staff can reach over the sterile area when wearing a sterile glove.
Sterile equipment should be handled only if the nurse is wearing a sterile glove.
Staff can touch the inside of the sterile contents wrapper with bare hands.

studentQuestionArray.content: A registered nurse is teaching a novice nurse about the guidelines for setting up a sterile field. Which statements made by a novice nurse are correct? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Limiting foot traffic while setting up a sterile field minimizes contamination and air currents.
Option 2:Staff in sterile garb should stay at least 1 foot away from nonsterile areas.
Option 3:The nurse should avoid reaching over the sterile area to prevent contamination, even if he or she is wearing a sterile glove.
Option 4:Handling sterile equipment with a sterile glove minimizes contamination.
Option 5:The inside of the wrapper is considered to be sterile. Therefore, the nurse should touch the inside of the wrapper only after donning the sterile glove.
